identity.title: Unified Idea → Brainstorm → Plan Document with Agent-Directive State Schemas
identity.summary: Merge the separate Idea row and PlanArtifact JSON into a single progressive document that advances through idea → brainstorming → planning → reviewed → accepted → delivered. Each state has a machine-readable schema with an agentDirective section that prescribes agent behavior for that state. The brainstorming state schema directs the full agent-led session: question sequence, scoring formulas, synthesis update. Introduces a Brainstorm dashboard button and Brainstorming rollup.

goals: A single document traces an idea from inception through brainstorming, planning, review, acceptance, and delivery — no separate entities to keep in sync
The document status advances through six states: idea → brainstorming → planning → reviewed → accepted → delivered
Each state has a machine-readable schema file with an agentDirective section that prescribes required agent behavior for that state — agents read the state schema and follow it, not playbook prose

The brainstorming state schema agentDirective defines the full agent-led session: ordered question phases, scoring sub-inputs, formula application steps, and synthesis update — agents must not invent a different flow
Agent-led brainstorming sessions are stored as an ordered array on the document and can be triggered at any document status
Each brainstorming session computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore using the canonical formulas defined in the schema
The dashboard surfaces a Brainstorm button and a Brainstorming rollup showing synthesized scores (Value, Risk, Effort, Confidence, Priority) for ideas in brainstorming state
Planning schema sections (WBS, goals, non-goals) are added to the same document when the operator advances to planning
Task refs and delivery refs attach to the unified document as work progresses to delivered
nonGoals: Maintaining a legacy two-artifact model (separate Idea SQLite row and PlanArtifact JSON) for any ideas after migration
Automated pipeline migration of existing ideas and plan artifacts — a lightweight manual agent task handles the low-volume cutover
Changing finalize-plan-to-phase task-creation logic as part of this tranche
Making agent directive weights runtime-configurable in v1 — weights are fixed in the schema; future iteration adds workspace-level overrides

userStories.iWant: to click Brainstorm on an idea card
userStories.soThat: an agent reads the brainstorming state schema and conducts the prescribed session — asking the right questions, computing scores, and updating the document — without me having to guide the process

userStories.iWant: to trigger a new brainstorming session on a document already in planning or later states
userStories.soThat: I can revisit assumptions and update synthesized scores without rolling back the document status

userStories.iWant: to see a Brainstorming rollup with Value, Risk, Effort, Confidence, and Priority scores
userStories.soThat: I can compare and prioritize all ideas currently in brainstorming state

userStories.iWant: my brainstorm sessions, plan WBS, task refs, and delivery refs to live on the same document
userStories.soThat: I never need to cross-reference a separate idea row and plan artifact file

userStories.iWant: to load the state schema for the current document state
userStories.soThat: I know exactly what to do next — which fields to populate, what questions to ask, what transitions are valid — without guessing or consulting playbook prose

userStories.iWant: existing ideas migrated to the unified format
userStories.soThat: there is no legacy split-model debt after the cutover

valueAssessment.impact: Eliminates split-model complexity; adds agent-directive schemas that make agent behavior deterministic and repeatable; structured scoring gives a defensible prioritization basis before committing to planning
valueAssessment.rationale: Schema design and scoring model are well-understood; integration risk exists at the planning module boundary and in correctly authoring directive schemas that cover all agent edge cases

riskAssessment.description: Hard cutover leaves existing ideas and plan artifacts inaccessible until migration task runs
riskAssessment.mitigation: Migration task runs dry-run first; existing volume is low and manageable by a single agent session

riskAssessment.description: Six-state status machine with per-state required sections and agentDirective is more complex than the previous two-state idea model
riskAssessment.mitigation: Per-state schema files make each state's contract explicit and machine-checkable; agents load the schema rather than inferring behavior

riskAssessment.description: Planning module commands are coupled to the two-artifact model and require non-trivial updates
riskAssessment.mitigation: WBS-3 adapts commands to the unified document plan section; external argv shapes are preserved

riskAssessment.description: Incomplete or ambiguous agentDirective in the brainstorming schema could cause agents to skip questions or compute scores incorrectly
riskAssessment.mitigation: WBS-1 acceptance criteria require the directive to be walkthrough-tested with a real agent session before sign-off

riskAssessment.description: Scoring formula weights may need calibration after real-world use
riskAssessment.mitigation: Weights are annotated in schema metadata; a future iteration adds workspace-level overrides without changing v1 defaults

systemsTouched: schemas/ideas/states/ (six per-state schema files with agentDirective sections)
schemas/ideas/ (unified IdeaPlan envelope schema, brainstorm-session record schema)
src/modules/ideas/ (six-state machine, commands, scoring engine, state-schema loader)
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon (dashboard rollup, button labels, idea detail panel, scoring display)
.ai/playbooks/brainstorm-session.md (new — human companion to brainstorming state schema)
technicalImpact.compatibilityNotes: Hard cutover — all existing I### rows and plan artifact JSON files must be migrated before the new dashboard code deploys.
technicalImpact.migrationImpact: Existing I### rows promoted to unified document format with correct state mapping; associated plan artifact JSON merged into corresponding unified documents. No legacy two-model path maintained after migration.

architecture.overview: The unified IdeaPlan document is a single progressive artifact with six status states: idea → brainstorming → planning → reviewed → accepted → delivered. Each state has a dedicated machine-readable schema file under schemas/ideas/states/ that defines (a) required/optional fields at that state, (b) valid transitions, and (c) an agentDirective section prescribing agent behavior. The brainstorming state schema agentDirective is the authoritative contract for the agent-led session. Agents load the state schema and follow the directive; they do not improvise. The brainstorm section stores an ordered sessions[] array appendable at any document state. The plan section is added at planning state. The Ideas module owns the document lifecycle. The Planning module is a section writer only.

decisions.decision: Hard cutover — no legacy two-model path after migration
decisions.rationale: Operator decision; low existing data volume makes this safe and removes long-term maintenance burden

decisions.decision: Six-state status machine: idea → brainstorming → planning → reviewed → accepted → delivered
decisions.rationale: Reviewed and Accepted are first-class document statuses; Delivered replaces finalized; each state maps to a schema file

decisions.decision: Per-state machine-readable schema files with agentDirective sections — agents must load and follow the state schema; it is authoritative over playbook prose
decisions.rationale: Eliminates agent improvisation and ensures reproducible, comparable sessions across runs

decisions.decision: Brainstorming sessions are an array on the document — start-brainstorm-session appends a session at any document state
decisions.rationale: Allows assumptions to be revisited without rolling back status; only first call from idea state transitions to brainstorming

decisions.decision: Scoring formulas fixed defaults in v1 with weights annotated in the brainstorming state schema for future override support
decisions.rationale: Consistent comparable scores for rollup ranking; weights stored in schema for discoverability

decisions.decision: Storage model (JSON blob on SQLite row vs linked artifact file) resolved in WBS-1
decisions.rationale: Both options viable; WBS-1 evaluates based on query and update access patterns

decisions.decision: Ideas module owns the document lifecycle; Planning module enriches the plan section only
decisions.rationale: Preserves clear module ownership and prevents circular dependency

uiUxDirection.summary: Replace the Plan button on idea cards with a Brainstorm button. Add a secondary Brainstorm button on idea detail panels for documents in any post-brainstorming state. Add a Brainstorming rollup (replacing the New plan rollup) showing synthesized Value, Risk, Effort, Confidence, and Priority scores for ideas in brainstorming state. Idea detail panel shows brainstorm session history with per-session and synthesized scores.

criticalPaths: Each per-state schema validates a fixture document at that state and rejects documents missing required fields for that state
Brainstorming state schema agentDirective walkthrough covers all 13 scoring sub-inputs and produces correct computed scores
Scoring engine produces correct outputs for all five score types given known inputs
Session synthesis applies 60/40 recency weighting correctly across multiple sessions
Brainstorm button transitions idea→brainstorming; secondary brainstorm appends session without status change
Brainstorming rollup displays correct synthesized scores from dashboard-summary projection
Planning commands write to and read from the unified document plan section correctly
outOfScopeTesting: E2E browser automation for the dashboard in v1 tranche

implementationGuidance: Start with WBS-1 — per-state schema files with agentDirective sections are the foundation everything else depends on
The brainstorming state schema agentDirective must be complete enough that an agent can run a full session from it alone; walkthrough-test with a real agent before marking WBS-1 done
Session synthesis: synthesizedScore=latestSession×0.60+mean(priorSessions)×0.40 per score type; embed verbatim in brainstorming state schema
State-schema loader in Ideas module must be a reusable utility — given a document status, returns the schema file path and parsed directive
Update Ideas module status machine (WBS-2) before any dashboard code (WBS-4)
Extend dashboard-summary projection to expose brainstorm.synthesis fields before updating the webview rollup render
whatNotToDo: Do not let agents invent question sequences or scoring logic — they must read and follow the brainstorming state schema agentDirective
Do not flatten brainstorm session records or plan sections into individual SQLite columns — use JSON blobs
Do not allow the Planning module to own the document lifecycle or create top-level documents
Do not deploy the Brainstorm button before the backend brainstorming status and session commands are live
Do not skip the recency-weighting step in synthesis — a flat average obscures recent reevaluation
Do not run migration without a dry-run step even though volume is low
assumptions: The agentDirective format in state schema files is JSON-serializable and loadable by the state-schema loader utility without additional tooling
All scoring sub-inputs are captured as 1–10 integer or decimal values by the agent during the session; the schema agentDirective specifies the prompt and valid range for each
effortTshirt maps cleanly to numeric anchor values (S=2,M=4,L=7,XL=10) for formula consistency
The existing planning command surface can be adapted to write to the unified document plan section without a complete rewrite
Existing ideas and plan artifacts are low-volume enough for a manual agent migration task with a dry-run step
WBS-1 resolves the storage model decision before WBS-2 and WBS-3 begin
openQuestions: OQ-1: Should the unified document be stored as a JSON blob on the SQLite ideas row, or as a linked artifact file following the existing plan-artifact file pattern? (Resolved in WBS-1)

wbs.title: Define per-state agent-directive schemas and unified IdeaPlan document schema
goalMapping: A single document traces an idea from inception through brainstorming, planning, review, acceptance, and delivery — no separate entities to keep in sync
Each state has a machine-readable schema file with an agentDirective section that prescribes required agent behavior for that state — agents read the state schema and follow it, not playbook prose

The brainstorming state schema agentDirective defines the full agent-led session: ordered question phases, scoring sub-inputs, formula application steps, and synthesis update — agents must not invent a different flow
Each brainstorming session computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore using the canonical formulas defined in the schema
US-5
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Define per-state agent-directive schemas and unified IdeaPlan document schema
wbs.approach: Produce six per-state schema files under schemas/ideas/states/ (idea, brainstorming, planning, reviewed, accepted, delivered). Each file defines: (a) required/optional fields at that state, (b) valid status transitions, (c) an agentDirective section. The brainstorming state schema agentDirective specifies the ordered question phases (context, value-scoring, risk-scoring, effort-scoring, confidence-scoring, unknowns, alternatives, session-notes), each question's field name, prompt text, type (score-1-10, text, enum), scoring guidance, and the compute and synthesis-update steps. Embed canonical scoring formula weights verbatim. Author the unified IdeaPlan envelope schema and brainstorm-session record schema. Decide and document the storage model. Produce four validation fixtures. Walkthrough-test the brainstorming state agentDirective with a real agent session before sign-off.
technicalScope: schemas/ideas/states/ (six per-state schema files with agentDirective sections)
src/modules/ideas/ (TypeScript types for unified document, session records, state directive shapes)
ADR or inline decision record for storage model
acceptanceCriteria: Six per-state schema files exist under schemas/ideas/states/ — each with required/optional fields, valid transitions, and an agentDirective section
The brainstorming state schema agentDirective covers all 13 scoring sub-inputs across ordered question phases with prompt text, valid range, and guidance for each
Canonical scoring formulas and weights are embedded verbatim in the brainstorming state schema agentDirective
Session synthesis formula (60/40 recency weighting) is embedded in the brainstorming state schema
All four progressive fixtures pass schema validation
TypeScript types compile clean
Storage model decision is documented
Brainstorming state agentDirective walkthrough-tested with a real agent session — agent completed all phases and computed correct scores from the directive alone
testingVerification: Unit test: each per-state fixture passes its state schema validation and fails when required fields are removed
Unit test: scoring engine produces correct outputs for known inputs as specified in the brainstorming state schema
Integration walkthrough: agent reads brainstorming state schema agentDirective cold and produces a complete, correct session record
wbs.riskNotes: The agentDirective authoring is the critical path — ambiguity here causes inconsistent agent behavior across sessions; budget time for the walkthrough-test AC
wbs.doneMeans: Six per-state schema files with agentDirectives committed; unified envelope and session schemas committed; four fixtures pass; scoring formulas embedded; storage model decided; brainstorming directive walkthrough-tested

wbs.title: Ideas module — six-state machine, state-schema loader, brainstorm session commands, and scoring engine
goalMapping: A single document traces an idea from inception through brainstorming, planning, review, acceptance, and delivery — no separate entities to keep in sync
The document status advances through six states: idea → brainstorming → planning → reviewed → accepted → delivered
Each state has a machine-readable schema file with an agentDirective section that prescribes required agent behavior for that state — agents read the state schema and follow it, not playbook prose

The brainstorming state schema agentDirective defines the full agent-led session: ordered question phases, scoring sub-inputs, formula application steps, and synthesis update — agents must not invent a different flow
Agent-led brainstorming sessions are stored as an ordered array on the document and can be triggered at any document status
Each brainstorming session computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore using the canonical formulas defined in the schema
US-1
US-2
US-5
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Ideas module: six-state machine, state-schema loader, brainstorm session commands, and scoring engine
wbs.approach: Implement the six-state status machine in the Ideas module. Build a reusable state-schema loader utility: given a document status, returns the schema file path and parsed directive for that state. Implement the scoring engine that reads formula weights from the brainstorming state schema and computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore. Implement session synthesis (60/40 recency weighting). Add start-brainstorm-session: transitions idea→brainstorming on first call; appends session on subsequent calls from any state. Add complete-brainstorm: validates brainstorm section and transitions to planning. Update create-idea, get-idea, update-idea for new statuses.
technicalScope: src/modules/ideas/ (six-state machine, state-schema loader utility, command handlers, scoring engine)
src/modules/ideas/instructions/ (new and updated command instruction .md files)
SQLite migration if storage model requires schema changes

Scoring engine reads formula weights from the brainstorming state schema (not hardcoded) and computes all five scores correctly
Session synthesis applies 60/40 recency weighting per score type

complete-brainstorm validates brainstorm section and transitions to planning

wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Planning module: write plan section to unified IdeaPlan document
wbs.approach: Update draft-plan-artifact, review-plan-artifact, accept-plan-artifact, and finalize-plan-to-phase to write to and read from the plan section of the unified document rather than a separate artifact file. review-plan-artifact transitions document status from planning to reviewed (using the reviewed state schema). accept-plan-artifact transitions from reviewed to accepted. finalize-plan-to-phase attaches task refs and transitions to delivered. Update start-idea-planning to initialize the plan section. Preserve external argv shapes.

wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Agent migration: promote existing Ideas and PlanArtifacts to unified document format
wbs.approach: Manual agent task. Read each existing I### row from SQLite. For rows with a linked plan artifact, read the plan artifact JSON and merge into the unified document plan section with status mapped correctly (planning, reviewed, accepted, or delivered based on existing artifact status). For rows without a linked plan artifact, promote to unified document format with status idea. Run dry-run first. Commit only after dry-run validates clean.

SQLite ideas table (write unified documents or promote existing rows)
acceptanceCriteria: All existing I### rows are in unified document format with correct status mapping after migration

Dry-run verified clean before commit — no data loss reported

testingVerification: Dry-run reports all ideas and their migration outcome before any writes

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 140
